sections.title: When talking to Sir Tiffy again

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what exactly do you want me to do?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Well now, it's really very simple. I want you to go to Sir Amik, tell him that you have decided to not join the Temple Knights, and that you have decided to become a White Knight instead.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: He will then offer you to join as a squire; you will then decline his offer, and ask if you can be deputised instead.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okaaaay....

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: If you run into any problems with that, come and see me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How will all that help?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't exactly understand how that will help me become a White Knight...

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Ah, your curiosity does you credit old boy! Let me tell you a little history lesson.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Many centuries ago, the founders of the White Knights realized that in times of crisis, a long waiting period for new Knights could be disastrous for the kingdom, so they added a clause to the founding charter that would

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: allow any citizen who has performed an act in the name of the White Knight to become deputised on a purely temporary basis to a full-fledged White Knight.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Well now, your actions in the Black Knights' Fortress on behalf of the White Knights make you eligible to be deputised.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Besides, you passed the trials to become a Temple Knight, what? If you're good enough to join the elite, then we'd not hesitate to deputise you into the White Knights.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But if deputisation's only temporary, and I have to be a 'proper' White Knight to then become a Temple Knight, doesn't that mean that I am still going to have to squire for Amik for five years?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Ah, well this is where the beauty of the plan lies, dontchaknow?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Once you have been deputised, for the time that you are still working for their order, you are subject to all of the same rules and regulations that normal White Knights are, which means you are also eligible for battlefield

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: promotions.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I... don't understand...

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Let me put it like this; As a deputy you will be considered the lowest possible rank of the White Knight, and may be removed entirely from their order at a whim.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: However, should you achieve some great feat while serving as a White Knight that you would be eligible for a promotion from, then by their own rules you would then have to be raised to a higher level within their order, and

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: it would therefore become impossible to remove your rank, or make you no longer a deputy.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So I get to be a White Knight without having to do five years of pointless busywork?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Indeed. We Temple Knights have our reputation for a reason, donchaknow?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I guess that makes sense... But we're not in a time of crisis, so doesn't that mean I won't actually get deputised?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Ah, do not concern yourself with THAT old [boy/gal]; Crisis is such a subjective term, and as you will find out when you join us fully in the Temple Knights, is always some potential crisis or other brewing.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: If we weren't as good at our jobs we were, almost every day would be a day of crisis!

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: All we really need to do is allow the old White Knights to handle a problem that we would normally deal with, and that should be a sufficient crisis to allow you to be deputised, donchaknow.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But... Do I really need to quit the Temple Knights

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You seem pretty cool, and I really like the armour...

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Haw Haw Haw!

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: No, not at all, this is merely a ruse; Sir Amik knows full well that his authority over our organization is limited, so he will not deputise you unless he thinks that you are entirely unaffiliated with us.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: You are simply going to tell him that you have decided not to join us so that he will in turn deputise you to join the White Knights! We of course still want you to join us, it is just a

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: little... misdirection.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What am I supposed to be doing again?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: You are to go to Sir Amik Varze, leader of the White Knights of Falador, and are to tell him that you have decided not to join the Temple Knights.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: We will send him a messenger confirming this. He will offer you the opportunity to become his squire, you are to decline, and ask if you can be deputised instead.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: If you run into any problems with that, you are then to come and see me for further advice.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Understood?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Um, yeah, I think so...

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: What ho, [sirrah/milady]. Jolly good show on the old training grounds thingy, what?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So Tiffy, now that I'm a Temple Knight Initiate do you have any jobs for me to do?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have any quests for a hard-working adventurer?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Well old [boy/gal/bean], looking at the old dossier we have on you here there seems to be a tiny problem, what?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Problem? What problem? Er... and you have a dossier on me???

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Well OF COURSE we have a dossier on you! We have a dossier on EVERYONE! It's our job, dontchaknow!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Um... okay... So what's the problem?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Well now, seems we made a tiny blunder when we first contacted you to join the Temple Knights, what?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Entirely our own fault, could have been avoided and all that, but a bit of a show-stopper, what?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Well [lad/lass], here's the thing: Only members of the White Knights may join the Temple Knights. By ancient decree, dontchaknow.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What? But I am a member of the White Knights! I went and did that stuff with the cabbage and the potion!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And I've already done your stupid little mind test thing! You can't get rid of me that easily!

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Calm yourself down [lad/lass]... We have no intention of 'getting rid of you', we are very keen on seeing you join us...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Then what's the problem?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Well, it's like this: Although you have performed a mission on behalf of the White Knights, you were never actually working for them.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: As I say, when our order was founded, we were expected to take the best of the best, and it was assumed that anyone worth having on the team would have been a White Knight already, what?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Sadly, this is not the case anymore, and the fact that you have to serve as a Squire to a White Knight for a minimum period of five years before you can actually become one tends to put the more short-attention spanned adventurers off joining, what?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You want me to be a squire for five years?!?!

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Haw Haw Haw! No, no, goodness me, no!

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Five years is a very long period of time nowadays – ANYTHING could happen in that time, and we need capable [men/women] like yourself as soon as possible.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: No, I had a bit of a chat with Sir Vey, and we came up with... A different solution to the problem. Assuming you're still interested, of course.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes I'm interested.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still up for joining the Temple Knights, I just don't really feel like spending five years running around shining peoples boots for them.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Haw Haw Haw!

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: I quite understand, what? No, don't worry, we were doing a little research into the White Knight Charter, and found an interesting loophole, what?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Loophole? What kind of loophole?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, you don't need to worry about the details, you just do as I tell you and it will all work out for us both, what?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I guess... I don't really like blindly following orders though...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I'm bored now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, to be honest I'm getting sick of all these excuses and procrastination. Plus being an initiate was nowhere near as fun as I thought it would be.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd rather just go off by myself and kill stuff for cash.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Well, sorry to hear you say that [boy/gal/bean], but I can't hold it against you.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: You will of course still be entitled to all that we can provide you with at an initiate level, but sadly you will never advance beyond that in our order. Was there anything you wanted?

sections.title: Falador Castle: First visit to Sir Amik
Talking to Sir Amik after refusing to become his squire

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Sir Amik.

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: Hello, friend!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Got any jobs for me to do today?

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: Only to become my Squire. Do you wish to become my squire?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, not really. So there isn't a crisis yet?

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: Thank Saradomin, no! We have not had a crisis for many years now!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right. Well, I'll go and see if Tiffy can fix that for me then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er, I mean: How fortunate for us all that nothing terrible has happened or is conveniently about to happen.

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: Indeed!

sections.title: Talking to Sir Amik after becoming his squire

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Sir. Amik

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: Hello Friend

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So do you have any squiring for me today Amik?

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: Yes, yes in fact I do. I would like you to bring me an Asgarnian Ale.

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: No matured Ale, no other brand, simply an Asgarnian Ale.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay boss.

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: That is 'Sir Amik' or 'My Liege' – not 'Boss'.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Gotcha, boss.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Sir. Amik

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: Hello Friend

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi boss.

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: Call me Sir Amik, or liege.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sure thing, boss. I brought you a beer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Anything else you want boss?

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: Well, another Asgarnian Ale would be greatly appreciated. And stop calling me Boss.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sure thing boss!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Sir. Amik

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: Hello Friend

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: Well, I did have something you may have been interested in, but I've been given orders not to approach you for trivial matters now that you are a member of the Temple Knights.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Aaaaawwwww...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: got any jobs for me to do today?

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: Well, I did have something you may be interested in, but I've been given orders not to-

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I just stop you right there?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am no longer a member of the Temple Knights, I have entirely renounced my application because after many hours of soul searching I have come to the conclusion that I desperately want to become a White Knight instead!

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: You don't want to be a Temple Knight anymore?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That is correct, yes.

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: But you are wearing their armour!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So? Did you hear me bad mouth their armour? The armour is cool, I kept the armour!

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: I'm not sure I understand... You wish to desert the Temple Knights in favour of my own humble White Knights?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Absolutely!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And this is definitely not some kind of elaborate hoax to get my application processed faster.

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: I'm sorry, did you say something just then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No... Not if you didn't hear it...

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: ...Well anyway, for the fine work you achieved at that incident in the Black Knights Fortress, I would be honoured to accept you as my squire.

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: So what do you say?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what exactly does being a squire consist of, then?

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: You will spend the next five years of your life running various errands for me.

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: If I consider you to have done a more than adequate job of this, then I will begin to consider possibly moving your name forwards to becoming a full-fledged member of the Order of the White Knights.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So... No killing stuff for you, or anything fun like that...?

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: Not as such, no. Unless you consider killing time by running errands fun...?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, erm...

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: So what say you Player? Spend five long years of your life doing pointless busywork without pay for me, on the off chance that at some point in the future I may put your name forward as a novice White Knight?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes please! I would LOVE to be your squire!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wait... Didn't Sir Tiffy tell me to decline...?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nah, Squire me up, Amik!

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: As you wish, from this day forth you are my Squire!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Um... As tempting an offer as that sounds Sir Amik, I am really not a fan of waiting around...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Now I heard from some completely random stranger that was definitely not Sir Tiffy Cashien as part of an elaborate ruse to fast track my application into the White Knights, that I could be deputised directly as a White

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Knight, and entirely bypass that while boring waiting for five year thing...

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: That is entirely correct [player name], but I have no idea where you might have heard that for it is a secret guarded only to members of our order!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right. Well, Can I do that instead?

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: Well, a fine adventurer would be a perfect candidate as a deputy in my eyes, but fortunately this can only be done in a time of crisis, and all is currently well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: All is well huh? I'll be right back, I just have to go ask...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: nobody in particular if they can organise...nothing at all to happen.

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: You are a strange one [player name]...

sections.title: Talking to Sir Tiffy after agreeing to be Amik's Squire

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I did it Tiffy! I became Sir Amik's squire!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That means in just five short years I can be a White Knight!

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: ...What? I told you to decline!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeeeesssssss.... But I thought it would be more fun to do this?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: *sigh* This is a less than illustrious start to your career with us [player name].

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Never mind, all is not lost, I have sent word to Amik that a dangerous criminal we once thought exiled is on the loose again, and that he should send all available White Knights to confront him.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: This should give him enough of a scare to deputise you immediately.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Go and speak to Amik again, and then immediately come back here, we will need to prepare you for your mission.

sections.title: Talking to Sir Tiffy after declining to be Amik's Squire

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again Tiffy. I've just spoken to Amik, and he says he's fine for me to be deputised as a White Knight, there just needs to be a crisis first.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I assume you can arrange a small crisis for me?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Haw Haw Haw, fitting right in with the Temple Knight way of thinking, what?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Well as a matter of fact, I will not have to organise anything, there is a particularly vicious and dangerous criminal currently at loose!

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: I have already notified Sir Amik through the usual channels, so just go and speak to him quickly, then pop back here and we'll get you equipped for the hunt, what?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You know... Is all this back and forth strictly necessary?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't mean to sound ungrateful for the help you're giving me here, but you could have just got this sorted out before sending me off there in the first place!

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Ah, yes, well most awfully sorry about all the running around you've been doing old bean, but when it comes to our line of work, sometimes the indirect route is the best, what?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't understand...?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And stop calling me bean! I am not a vegetable, I am a human bean, er, being!

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Awfully sorry dear heart, it's just a term of affection, what? Anyway...

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: This criminal is a very dangerous fellow, and has some very dangerous friends.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: If he has no reason to suspect you to be working for us, then he will be a little sloppier than he otherwise might be, because he will just assume you to be one of those stupid White Knights.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: We want this man caught, for the good of the country, and if we need to fool a few people into thinking you're nothing to do with us, and you have to do a little extra walking, then that's what is going to happen, what?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I guess... But this spy stuff is a lot more boring than it sounded at first...

sections.title: Talking to Sir Tiffy before revisiting Sir Amik

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi Tiffy. What am I supposed to be doing again?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Go and report to Sir Amik for 'orders'. You will be unsurprised to find out he will now be prepared to deputise you.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: After you have spoken to him return here immediately, your life will be at risk without our assistance, and we don't want you dying on your first mission for us, what?

sections.title: Falador Castle: Second visit to Sir Amik
Talking to Sir Amik after agreeing to go after Solus

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Sir Amik.

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: Hello, friend!

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: [Player]... Solus Dellagar is still loose... Please, for the safety of my lands you must find him immediately...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any hints on where he might be?

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: We have no idea where he might be now... His magic is too powerful for us, I do not know how to stop him...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hum. I wonder if Sir Tiffy might be able to help?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Speak about the Wanted! Quest

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: Thank Saradomin you have come back here!

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: We have received word from one of our scouts that the evil Solus Dellagar has returned to Asgarnia!

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: What is worse, it seems he is in possession of dangerous new magics we are powerless against!

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: Please, I implore you in the name of all that is holy, assist us in banishing him from our kingdom once more!

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: I cannot bear to think of the troubles he may bring us...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Solus Dellagar? Who - or what - is that?

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: You have not heard of the infamous murder-mage? He terrorised these lands many years past, thinking himself above the law with his powerful enchantments, but the White Knights finally managed to bring him to

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: justice and he was permanently exiled.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Exiled? Why didn't you just kill him? Or, you know, imprison him or whatever?

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: Kill an unarmed foe? That is not the way of Saradomin, nor of the White Knights.

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: As for imprisonment...Solus is a highly dangerous man not just because of his mastery of the magical arts, but for his uncanny resourcefulness.

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: To tell you the truth, I fear that I would be unable to guarantee his captivity in any holding cell belonging to us...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So if you can't imprison him, and exile obviously didn't work, you want me to kill him?

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: I did not say that. I did not say that at all. That is against all of our codes of honour.

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: However, should you find yourself in combat with him, and there is no other way, then perhaps for him to die would be best for us all.

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: You understand me, [player name]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh yes. You've made yourself very clear.

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: So will you aid us in this fight?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what will I get if I help you out against this, er, solus doolalala guy then?

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: Why, you will help keep Asgarnia free of malicious influence, and perform a good deed in the name of King Vallance and of the White Knights!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, yes I see. So...No monetary compensation or other actual reward then?

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: As Saradomin has told us, 'a good deed is its own reward'.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So I don't actually get anything out of this then?

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: Nothing tangible, no, but you will be deputized to act as a White Knight, and you will feel a warm glow inside of having done the right thing.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But you can't buy things with a warm glow...

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: So what say you [player name]? Fight with honour in the name of the White Knights against the terrible Solus?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey, there's nothing I like more than fighting! Deputise me up, and I'll go get this guy for you!

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: Very well then; In the name of King Vallance, and for the honour of Saradomin, I now pronounce you a fully deputised White Knight.

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: May honour and glory follow you in every action.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So where should I start looking for this Solus guy anyway?

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: I haven't the faintest idea. Good Luck!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A scary mage huh? Sounds like I could get KILLED!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Forget that and forget you! No way am I risking my neck unless there is a big fat reward on the table for it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And sometimes not even then...

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: It is a tragedy to hear you speak so, but we must trust in our valour and the wisdom of Saradomin to protect us from this evil man if we can not enlist help against him.

sections.title: Talking to Tiffy after being told what you need for the Communication Orb but before making it

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi Tiffy. What am I supposed to be doing again?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Bringing me either cash or items so that I can equip you for your mission correctly.

sections.title: Claiming another Communication Orb from Tiffy

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey Tiffy, I lost my CommOrb...

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Very careless of you, what? Here, take another, but be careful of this one!

sections.title: Talking to Tiffy before using the Communication Orb

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi Tiffy. What am I supposed to be doing again?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: You are hunting for Solus Dellagar, the evil criminal mastermind.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: I suggest you contact Savant through your CommOrb and see if she can send you any relevant information about your assignment.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello...?

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Well, hello! You must be [player name]? Sir Tiffy told me to expect you to call in on this frequency!

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: So what do you think? Pretty nifty little piece of kit this crystal, huh?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah, it must be very useful.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Oh, trust me you'll find out exactly how useful! Now, was there something you wanted?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi Savant! I was wondering if you could give me details on my current assignment?

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Okaaaaay... Bringing up your current assignment details...

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Oh! You're chasing Solus Dellagar?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh yeah! So any information you can give me?

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Listen [player name]. Solus is an incredibly dangerous criminal, and I don't think his grasp on reality is too tight, if you understand what I'm saying.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: You really need to take care with this one. I'm amazed you got an assignment as tough as this one as a first assignment to be honest!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, how would you suggest I go about finding him?

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Well, this CommOrb can use the scan function to find him, but its range is very limited.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So I have to just wander around the place looking for him?

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: No, that would take far too long... Listen, I am going to transfer all information on Solus that we currently have to your CommOrb.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: You should be able to find a hint of where to find him by examining it.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: The Library tells me that we have two visual recordings from SpyOrbs available to you, as well as a standard dossier.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: I would suggest the people that the SpyOrbs were observing, when they picked up records of Solus, would be a good place to start your investigation.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who are these people then?

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Okay just checking now...

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Record one is from a routine monitoring of Lord Daquarius.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: In case you haven't heard of him, he is the leader of the Kinshra, also known as Black Knights.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: As a major enemy of the White Knights we have had him under observation for some time now, so it was pretty lucky that Solus decided to visit him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So where would I find this Lord Daquarius?

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: He has a secret Black Knight training ground deep in the heart of Taverley dungeon, along with holding facilities for prisoners who they wish to interrogate later.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: He is almost certain to be found thereabouts.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And what is Record 2 about?

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Just checking... Ah yes.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: This shady fellow is a member of the Zamorakian Magical Institute.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: We suspect them to be the group responsible for destroying the Wizards Tower all of those years ago, as well as other more recent acts of sabotage.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: We also suspect that they have been responsible for a number of thefts of a mysterious rock that the Wizards Tower has started producing, but don't have much intelligence about that.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: We set up an observation SpyOrb in a known Zamorakian shrine in south-east Varrock, but he moves between there and the wilderness just north of Edgeville quite frequently.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: He doesn't seem much of a threat, but we placed him under observation anyway, which seems to have paid off by getting some more information about Solus.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: From the look of the record, he would be worth talking to as well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks Savant!

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Any time, [player name]. Over and out.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So... Savant... That's an unusual name...

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Well, obviously it's not my real name!

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: My real name is Elyssa Van Lendes, but for operational purposes I am called Savant.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what exactly do you do for the Temple Knights?

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: I am the S.M.R. and Chief Librarian for the Temple Knights.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: S.M.R? What's that?

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: It stands for Senior Mystical Researcher. The Temple Knights have a world class research team investigating new types of magic constantly. As senior researcher, and liaison, I can pass on information to the rest of the Temple Knights.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Many of the devices used by the Temple Knights such as our CommOrbs and SpyOrbs were first designed by my team as experimental devices, and have since become standard field equipment!

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: I have always had a gift for magic, and have spent most of my life trying to find applications for it that would serve our order and give us an edge over our opponents.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your opponents?

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Oh yes, don't think for a second that we are the only secret organisation in Gielinor!

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: There are many factions in these lands, some almost as magically advanced and organised as ourselves!

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: It is a constant struggle to keep our operatives with advanced magical equipment that our opponents don't have - this is why we have the self-destruct safeguards, to prevent our equipment ever falling into unfriendly hands.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So how did you end up working for the Temple Knights?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I would have thought someone like you would have ended up researching at the Wizards' Tower or something instead...

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Oh yes... The Wizards' Tower...

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Let me tell you something, the mages who work there are idiots! They told me that magical research wasn't suitable for 'just a girl' and that I would be 'too scared' to research for them effectively!

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Luckily the Temple Knights are far more open minded about things, and I really feel like my work is making a difference to them!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, thanks Savant, that was very interesting.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Hey, we're all Temple Knights here, right? Glad I could help, [player name]

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Savant over and out.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi Savant. I was just wondering if you could give me a little information about the Temple Knights?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've just realised that I really don't know very much about them...

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Well, you are not officially a full fledged Temple Knight now, are you Initiate [player name]?

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: I am afraid that means for security reasons I can only give you a brief overview of the organisation...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, that's better than nothing right?

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: That is usually the case, yes, I agree. Well, let's see if I remember how this goes... 'Legends tell us that Lord Saradomin once walked these lands amongst us, and delivered his wisdom unto us personally, so that we could grow wise in his honour.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: But then came the war, that shattered our lands, and the interloper banished our land from us!

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: But the wisdom of Saradomin knew that such events might occur, and he laid plans in secret amongst his most devout followers!

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Those warriors whose hearts were as pure as their minds were sharp were called to him, and were made into his Temple Knights, for they were the Knights that guarded his very first Temple.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: All who dwelled in his lands knew that the Temple Knights were the honoured, the blessed, the personally chosen warriors of Saradomin, and thus were they given great freedom and authority in all of our Lords lands.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: But with Saradomin no longer with us, the Temple Knights were no longer honoured and respected by all!

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Rather, they were made targets by Saradomins enemies, and driven from the lands unjustly!

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Thus it was that they became a secret force, hidden from the eyes of Saradomins enemies, that could strike against evil in his name swiftly and quietly whenever they may be needed.'

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Something like that I think, it's been a long time since I heard it last...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks Savant.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tiffy, it worked exactly to plan! Sir Amik deputised me to take care of this Solus Doolally dude, so let's go get him!

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: His name is Solus Dellagar, and honestly, he is one of the most dangerous men on the planet.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Not only is he extremely adapt at magic, he is also quite prepared to use that magic at a moments notice, because he is quite, quite mad.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So... Dragonhide armour and an elemental shield and I'm laughing?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: No, no, no. Listen to me [boy/girl]; You are under NO CIRCUMSTANCES to engage him directly in combat!

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: You are to act as our agent and track him down, when you have managed to corner him somewhere then we will send in a field recovery team directly to take him away without incident.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: I cannot stress this enough; You are to observe and locate, you are NOT to enter a combat situation!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Riiiiight.... So I'm just going to scout him out for you then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any tips on how to find him?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Well old [boy/girl], we need to equip you for the task, but you'll need to get something for me first, what?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What kind of stuff?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Well, we teach our operatives the value of their equipment by making them pay for it, what?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: You have two choices here, you can either give me the cash directly, and I will hand it over, or you can bring me the raw materials and I will make you one.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Well we in the Temple Knights have some specific equipment for situations such as this dontchaknow, but you'll need to free up some inventory space first!

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: It's 10,000 coins for the Temple Knight Communication Orb. You have that kind of money with you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh yeah, I got your money.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Then you wish to purchase a Temple Knight Communication Orb?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Here take this Communications Orb, or 'CommOrb' for short.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: It is a precise magical mechanism that we can use to track him, by sending data of the surrounding area back to one of our information operatives at the Temple Knight Headquarters.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Your information operative is known as Savant, I suggest you contact them using your CommOrb at your earliest convenience.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: They will be able to send you any useful data about Solus directly through it for your perusal, dontchaknow.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, thanks Tiffy!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, no thanks.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: As you wish, what?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: It's 10,000 coins for the Temple Knight Communication Orb. You have that kind of money with you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't have that kind of money with me...

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Perhaps you would like to just bring me the components of it then, what?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Bring me one law rune, one slayer gem and one piece of molten glass and I will make you a Communication Orb.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I give you the components I have with me now?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Heavens, boy! I am not a pawn shop, to be holding bits and pieces for you while you go off and do something else!

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: No, no you bring me all three components at the same time, what?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay Tiffy.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Bring me one Law Rune, one Slayer Gem and one piece of Molten Glass and I will make you a Communication Orb.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I give you the components I have with me now?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Heavens, boy! I am not a pawn shop, to be holding bits and pieces for you while you go off and do something else!

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: No, no you bring me all three components at the same time, what?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have all three components for you to make me a Communication Orb out of now Tiffy.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Excellent! You wish me to make you one now then, what?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, no thanks.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: As you wish, what?

lines.speaker: Solus Dellagar
lines.text: Lord Daquarius! Still festering in your pathetic little dungeon, I see.

lines.speaker: Lord Daquarius
lines.text: Solus. I thought you dead many moons past...

lines.speaker: Solus Dellagar
lines.text: Ha! Wishful thinking, perhaps?

lines.speaker: Lord Daquarius
lines.text: Cut the banter, Solus. What do you want? You know you are not welcome here.

lines.speaker: Solus Dellagar
lines.text: Now is that any way to talk to a fellow Zamorakian?

lines.speaker: Lord Daquarius
lines.text: You are not a Zamorakian! You worship nothing but your own selfish greed.

lines.speaker: Solus Dellagar
lines.text: Ah... Such mistrust of your forgotten ally! Why did I ever leave these lands?

lines.speaker: Lord Daquarius
lines.text: You left because your own stupidity brought the wrath of the Temple Knights upon you. Now I ask you a final time before I draw my blade: WHAT do you WANT?

lines.speaker: Solus Dellagar
lines.text: Oh... nothing much. I just thought you would like to know that I have returned as your new master.

lines.speaker: Lord Daquarius
lines.text: The Kinshra are not your personal soldiers Solus! I will not waste any of my warriors in your foolish schemes!

lines.speaker: Solus Dellagar
lines.text: And that is your final answer Daquarius?

lines.speaker: Lord Daquarius
lines.text: Leave. Now. Or there will be blood shed between us.

lines.speaker: Solus Dellagar
lines.text: You would threaten Solus Dellagar? Have you forgotten just who I am? Perhaps you need reminding!

lines.speaker: Solus Dellagar
lines.text: Still alive Daquarius? Good! I won't have to remind you of my power again next time! Make sure you are more prepared to follow orders next time I come to your little Taverley hideout!

lines.speaker: Lord Daquarius
lines.text: I swear... in the name of *cough* Zamorak... I will see you rot for this Solus...

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: What? Who dares... Oh no. Oh no, no, no.

lines.speaker: Solus Dellagar
lines.text: Well, what have we here? A shrine to Zamorak in the middle of Varrock?

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: S-S-Solus???

lines.speaker: Solus Dellagar
lines.text: I assume my reputation precedes me!

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: Please. Please don't kill me. I have no riches, I am but a magical researcher...

lines.speaker: Solus Dellagar
lines.text: Kill you? HAH HAH HAH! My dear fellow, if I wanted you dead, you ALREADY WOULD BE!

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: I... I don't know w-what you want Solus...but whatever it is, please, just t-take it and leave, please.

lines.speaker: Solus Dellagar
lines.text: Well I am particularly interested in your brains at the moment! But you wouldn't want me taking those and leaving, now would you?

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: My b-brains? I... I don't understand...

lines.speaker: Solus Dellagar
lines.text: I am interested in your research!

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: My... r-research? I c-cannot let you have my research it is being done in order to assist my lord Zamorak.

lines.speaker: Solus Dellagar
lines.text: Ah! But he is not here, is he?

lines.speaker: Solus Dellagar
lines.text: Ah-ah-ah!

lines.speaker: Solus Dellagar
lines.text: You are going nowhere just yet sir.

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: P-please I meant no disrespect Solus, please do not kill me...

lines.speaker: Solus Dellagar
lines.text: With your most interesting research! No, I will be content with simply taking your money sir!

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: Take it, take it all!

lines.speaker: Solus Dellagar
lines.text: Well a most productive trip on all accounts! I will see you again...Very soon!

sections.title: Talking to Lord Daquarius again before killing any knights

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Lord Daquarius
lines.text: What want you, with the Lord of the Kinshra? Speak!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I still want to know the whereabouts of Solus Dellagar!

lines.speaker: Lord Daquarius
lines.text: Well you will not hear that from me, White Knight dog!

lines.speaker: Lord Daquarius
lines.text: And you can threaten me all you want, I care for nothing in this world save the welfare of my Kinshra brothers!

lines.speaker: Lord Daquarius
lines.text: Stop! I will tell you what you want! Please... leave my men be...

sections.title: Talking to Lord Daquarius after killing a Black Knight

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Lord Daquarius
lines.text: What want you with lord of Kinshra? Speak!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok Daquarius, you tell me the whereabouts of Solus Dellagar right now, or I will put every Black Knight here to their death in front of you!

lines.speaker: Lord Daquarius
lines.text: *Sigh* I should have known the White Knights would be my ruin once again...

lines.speaker: Lord Daquarius
lines.text: I do not know his exact whereabouts, and when last we met we did not leave...on the best of terms.

lines.speaker: Lord Daquarius
lines.text: All I know is that he left behind some fur when he left, I would expect him to be in an area with furred creatures of some sort.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What king of fur? Dog? Wolf? Bear?

lines.speaker: Lord Daquarius
lines.text: I do not know, I am a warrior, not a zoo keeper. It was not bear fur, I know that much.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's it? That's your help? You'd better not be lying Daquarius, or...

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Calm yourself Player, he is telling the truth.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do you know?

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: I am monitoring your conversation through the CommOrb, his physiology shows none of the changes we usually get when someone lies to us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So this CommOrb thing can work as a lie-detector? That's pretty useful!

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Yes, it is, but back to the task at hand. I suggest you head to the next subject of our observations, he might be more help in locating Solus.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You mean that Zamorakian mage in Varrock?

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Yes, he would seem a likely bet. Savant out.

sections.title: Talking to Lord Daquarius after learning about Solus

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Lord Daquarius
lines.text: What want you, with the Lord of the Kinshra? Speak!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay Daquarius, tell me all that you know of Solus, or I will kill some more Black Knights!

lines.speaker: Lord Daquarius
lines.text: I have already told you, I know nothing except that he left some fur behind.

lines.speaker: Lord Daquarius
lines.text: Now leave me and my men be, or it will be the worse for you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Lord Daquarius
lines.text: What want you with lord of Kinshra? Speak!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am here on behalf of the White Knights...

lines.speaker: Lord Daquarius
lines.text: Pah! Begone, fool, or prepare to taste my blade!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Now wait a minute, this concerns a friend of yours....

lines.speaker: Lord Daquarius
lines.text: A friend of mine? What friend of mine would the Temple Knights show any intere-

lines.speaker: Lord Daquarius
lines.text: Oh. This... Is about Solus, isn't it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Bingo.

lines.speaker: Lord Daquarius
lines.text: Then let me assure you, that monster is no 'friend' of mine - if indeed such a creature even has 'friends'!

lines.speaker: Lord Daquarius
lines.text: No, that man is certainly no friend of mine nor the Kinshra.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you will help me find him?

lines.speaker: Lord Daquarius
lines.text: No fool... You and your White Knight cronies are no friend of the Kinshra either.

lines.speaker: Lord Daquarius
lines.text: I will help neither of you, and I wish you all a slow and painful death.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You know...I'm sure I could MAKE you tell me where he is...

lines.speaker: Lord Daquarius
lines.text: Is that a threat, whelp? Do your worst. I care not for my own life, I stand only for the protection of the Kinshra!

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Player? I've been monitoring you. He's telling the truth, so don't try to bluff him by starting a fight.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: All of our records show that the only thing he will make a stand for is the protection of his men, and he has little regard for his own safety in comparison.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: You're going to have to find some other way to intimidate him into giving you the information we need.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Understood. Player out.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you have information about the whereabouts of Solus Dellagar?

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: I do indeed. My price for this information is 20 pieces of rune essence.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay. I'll go get those for you then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have some essences with me, if you would take those...?

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: I will not haggle you on my price. 20 Rune essences, and not a single essence less.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll go get those for you then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have your essences right here, take them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, now you have your essence, give me my information.

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: Certainly. As I say, I have little information, but the cost was not too dear, was it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Get on with it, I haven't got all day to waste.

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: As you wish. As a powerful magician, I could sense his aura once I had encountered him, and it definitely moved East of here when he left me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He went East. That's it? That's your 'information'?

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: I am sorry I cannot be more specific, but that is all of the information I have to offer...

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Okay, so Daquarius told us he was probably somewhere with furry animals of some kind, and this mage has told us that he is East of here...

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Any ideas? Frankly, I'm stumped. I just don't have enough information to work out where he might be...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmmm. Wolf fur... and the East... I know where he is.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: You do?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He will be in Canifis. He must be.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Wow, that's some good thinking [Player name]! Okay, let's head there and get him!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am looking for information about a very dangerous man... I believe he visited you quite recently.

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: Ah, you mean that accursed Solus!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, him. I need to find him and make sure he doesn't come back to these lands.

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: You are working for the Temple Knights correct?

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: No, don't deny it, I recognise the magical signature of your Communication Orb, a very nice piece of equipment indeed.

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: I will tell you what I know in exchange for it, I believe such a device could be very useful to my research...

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: [Player name], I've been monitoring your conversation; You can't let him have Temple Knight equipment, he is a Zamorakian!

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Even if we wanted to let the ZMI have access to our research, the built in security magics would destroy it before trading anyway!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, I cannot trade you my Communications Orb...

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: Ah well, it was worth a try, but I did not seriously expect you to part with it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Then perhaps you will be willing to trade something else for your information...?

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: I have little information, and would give it freely to make sure Solus is... disposed of... but unfortunately I am somewhat... financially distraught at the moment due to a recent... incident.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You mean Solus robbed you of all of your money?

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: ...Yes. He took a large degree of my research into various things as well, but my research is inside my mind and easily replaceable, the money I possessed was essential for my experimentation, and as I have no real skills to speak of, is not so easily replaceable...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You want me to pay you for information?

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: No... not as such. You know of the substance known as 'rune essence' yes?

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: A small number of those will allow me to create runes from them, which I may then sell and get myself back on my feet.

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: Twenty parts of rune essence is the price for my information. You may take it or leave it...

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Hi [Player name], it sounds like a pretty good deal for this kind of information to me, but it's your call. We REALLY need to find Solus as soon as possible though, who knows what kind of damage he might cause now he is at large again!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll see what I can do.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Hello there [player name]. What can I help you with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi Savant. Where am I supposed to look for Solus again?

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: The clue was that Solus is somewhere where you might find Fur, East of Varrock...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nothing thanks Savant, I was just checking this thing still worked!

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: You shouldn't really waste my time like this, [Player]...

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Still no harm done, I guess! Call me if you need me!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay Savant, will do.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Savant out.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Ok Player, I'm going to begin a low level scan for Solus now...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any signs?

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Hmmm... These readings are very strange...

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: There's... SOMETHING here... You know, I'm sure I've seen readings like this somewhere before...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's wrong?

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Just give me a minute to cross reference the records in the Library. I know that I've seen readings like this before...

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: GAH! This is infuriating! It's on the tip of my tongue...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, that Zamorakian mage mentioned that he had a strong magical aura, perhaps that is it?

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: No...no, it is something...

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Oh my! I know what it is!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Savant...?

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Many years ago the Temple Knights were studying teleportation techniques, and found a strange dimension that was accessed when teleporting..

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: We stopped the research as it was too dangerous to be practically useful, but our initial studies showed that we could use the entire dimension as a kind of scrying glass to see a precise point where we wished to teleport to, then teleport extremely slowly between two points.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How is teleporting really slowly particularly useful?

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Well, basically, you are stretched so thinly between dimensions that you effectively become invisible!

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: From your perspective you can see three places simultaneously, and can choose to move between all three at will!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm... not sure I follow...

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Let me give you an example: If you were in Falador castle, and wished to teleport to Varrock, then you could use this 'slow-teleport' technique to be in both places simultaneously, but invisible to anyone in either.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: You could also move freely between the two invisibly and then choose which place you wished to end up, simply by concentrating on the location you wanted to appear in.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Needless to say, in our line of work this could come in very handy for sneaking around unobserved...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But you abandoned this research? Why?

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: It is far too dangerous. In my example I only used two places, but in practice there is a third place involved. It is a strange, empty dimension that teleports seem to pass through.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Frankly, the human mind isn't equipped to cope with processing so much information simultaneously, and the test subjects started to display... behavioral problems...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er... That doesn't sound good...

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: No, it wasn't: Aggressiveness was highly increased, and there were numerous perceptual faults, hallucinations and the like.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: We ceased experimentation immediately, there are enough things in this world to haunt a Temple Knight, we did not see the need to create anymore.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But this signature you are finding is the same as this 'slow-teleport' experiment created?

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: No... it is not exactly the same...

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: But it is close enough to be a cause of concern.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Either someone else has found this strange dimension and begun their own research, and reached the same conclusions that we did...

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Or... We have had a security leak within the Temple Knights and abandoned research has been sold to our enemies!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I can see how that might be a cause for concern, but right now our priority is Solus, right?

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Yes [player name], you're right. But if he is using this 'slow-teleport' technique, then that means it is going to be a lot harder to catch him!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Because he is invisible and in three separate places simultaneously?

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Exactly. Listen, I might be able to block his teleportation magics using your CommOrb, but I will need you to get close to him, and if we're unlucky then he will end up teleporting to the point away from you instead of appearing in front of you

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, we have nothing else to try, right? What will I have to do?

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Use the 'Scan' function of your CommOrb, if he is in the vicinity it should knock him out of his 'in-between' state, and make him appear.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So I should try a scan here in Canifis?

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: That would seem best... Savant over and out.

lines.speaker: Solus Dellagar
lines.text: What the...?

lines.speaker: Solus Dellagar
lines.text: Well well well! I was wondering when the White Knights would come looking for me!

lines.speaker: Solus Dellagar
lines.text: Well you'll have to be quicker than that, my friend!

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Argh!

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: He got away...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what can we do now?

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Wait a second...

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: There was another item in the teleport with him...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What does that mean?

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Well, I might be able to retrieve it from the slow-teleport!

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: It might give us a clue as to the second location Solus is in!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well what are you waiting for? Try and get it!

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: It's a beer?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I doubt he's getting drunk in hiding...

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: That type of beer is only sold in one place that I know of

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: It's...a wizard hat?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So he is improving his dress sense you think?

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: I think it's more likely he happens to be in an area where there are many wizards around.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: It's... ear muffs?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why on gielinor would he be wearing those?

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Well, they are standard equipment for any slayer looking to kill Banshees...

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: It's... goblin armour?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Goblin armour? Why would he wear that?

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Well, it must be a place here there is a lot of it around for it to have been caught in his teleport...

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: It's...a fake beard?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What the...? You think he is in disguise somewhere?

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: I've seen this design of beard before... But I cannot remember where...

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: It's... fur?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fur? How does that help us find him?

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Well, this is not fur directly from an animal, look it has been prepared for sale...

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: It's... an eye patch?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you think he might be injured?

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: No, I think he must be somewhere where there are a lot of people who wear eye patches...

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: It's... a hat?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How does a hat help us?

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: I'm sorry, I don't know. Maybe somewhere where hats like this are common?

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: It's... a ticket?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am sure I have seen tickets like that somewhere before...

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Well, wherever it is that you have seen them, I would guess he is nearby!

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: It's... a cloak?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Very stylish, but it doesn't seem to be his colour.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: I don't think he would have been wearing it... But he might be nearby somewhere that sells this type of cloak.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: It's... a toy horse?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What a weird thing to be carrying...

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Well [player name], bear in mind that it might just be an object that as in the vicinity rather than something he was carrying...

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: It's... some big frog legs?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The frog those came from must be huge!

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: I have heard rumours of giant frogs in a subterranean area...

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: It's... an unholy symbol!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The symbol of Zamorak? That tells us nothing.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Yes, sadly there are many Zamorakians in these lands...But it must be somewhere of special significance...

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: It's... a worm?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe he's doing a little fishing?

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: No, this is a very specific worm that is only found in one place...

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: It's... a banana?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A banana? How is that a clue to where he is?

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Well, for it to have got caught in his teleport, it must be a place where there are many around...

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: [player name], I want you to search for him, and use your Comm-Orb to scan for him if you think he is nearby!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay I'll head off immediately. Let's hope we're not too late.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: There doesn't seem to be anything unusual about these readings Player... Looks like your hunch was wrong. Savant out.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Negative result, Player. Looks like you're barking up the wrong tree. Savant out.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Scan results are negative, Player. Savant out.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Background magical levels all seem within normal tolerable limits Player...

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: I don't think this can be the right place... Savant out.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Checking incoming data...

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Nope, all clear here. Savant out.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Nope... I'm not picking up anything unusual near here... Savant out.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Analysis is negative I'm afraid, Player. Savant out.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: The background magical levels are slightly above average here, but not enough to signify anything.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: I think you're in the wrong place. Savant out.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Results are all-clear, Player. Savant out.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Not picking up any unusual readings here, Player... Savant out.

lines.speaker: Solus Dellagar
lines.text: You again???

lines.speaker: Solus Dellagar
lines.text: I warn you, if you interfere with my plans I will see you suffer!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Solus! I am here by authority of the White Knights of Falador!

lines.speaker: Solus Dellagar
lines.text: Ha! Like I care!

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Okay, I am attempting to block his teleport...

lines.speaker: Solus Dellagar
lines.text: Oh, we have a little magical skill do we?

lines.speaker: Solus Dellagar
lines.text: Then let's see how you deal with this!

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Argh!

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: I should have known he would try something like that!

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: I'm sorry [player name], I should have stopped him using that spell...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That doesn't matter right now Savant, did you get a reading of where his teleport was coming from?

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: The spell is still running, wait a moment... There!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well?

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: It's a cape...

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: I'm not sure how much help in tracking him that is.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: More than you might think Savant. This type of cape used to be called the Cape of Champions...

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Well I still don't know what help that is, but if you have an idea here he might be you should head there immediately!

lines.speaker: Solus Dellagar
lines.text: Well now, I am beginning to get sick of your constant interference!

lines.speaker: Solus Dellagar
lines.text: Or at least I would be if you weren't so laughably easy to escape from.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: *cough* Savant? *cough* I can't... *cough*

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He threw some kind of smoke at me or something...

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Don't worry about it [player name], he may have escaped AGAIN, but I still have him on my tracking spell...

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Okay, the smoke's cleared, and you don't seem to have been damaged by it!

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: You're lucky I think, readings show it was highly poisonous!

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Let's see if I grabbed anything useful from him while he was teleporting...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'm on my way. This Solus guy is really beginning to get on my nerves!

lines.speaker: Solus Dellagar
lines.text: This is getting very tiresome!

lines.speaker: Solus Dellagar
lines.text: Little White Knight, I command powers beyond your imagination!

lines.speaker: Solus Dellagar
lines.text: If you do not cease pursuing me, I will be forced to use them upon you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You can't threaten me old man...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Dead or alive, you're coming with me.

lines.speaker: Solus Dellagar
lines.text: This charade bores me!

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: [player name] I am reading a huge power surge from Solus, be very careful...

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: [player name]!!!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ow! What happened?

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: I managed to shield you from most of his attack, and bring you back to Falador Castle, but you are lucky I was monitoring, that could have killed you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't understand though Savant, you said he could only be in a few places simultaneously...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But he keeps getting away! And he keeps moving around!

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: You're right [player name], I don't know how he is doing it...

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: But he can't run forever, and every time he tries something against you, I can update your CommOrb to prevent it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So he isn't going to shoot me like that again?

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: No, [player name]. I am sorry that I let him hurt you like that...

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: But keep chasing him, together we are going to bring him to justice!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're right about that, there's no way I'm letting him get away with treating me like that!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So did we get any clues to his real location from that last scan?

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: That's the spirit [player name]! Just awaiting the data to come through...

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: It's... a very strange weapon?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I recognise that...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's the type of spear used by the Dorgeshuun goblins.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: The who? I've never heard of them before...

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Well, if you know who or what they are, you should go and see them, Solus must be nearby!

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: You should probably take a minute to restock on supplies and heal up though [player name].

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: I know we can find him and bring him to justice, but we don't want you dying while doing so!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay Savant, I'll heal up, then go looking for him amongst the Dorgeshuun.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: Oh thank you, you have freed me!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: W-what? Who are you?

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: Oh, I am but a poor maiden kidnapped by the evil Solus!

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: But you chased him so hard, that now I am free...

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: [player name], I am getting some strange readings from this 'maiden'...

lines.speaker: Solus Dellagar
lines.text: Muhahaha! Sucker!

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Sorry [player name], I should have spotted his little trick earlier...

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Are you okay? That looked like a nasty sucker punch...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oooohhhh...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Did anyone get the number of that wagon?

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Don't worry [player name], I am reconfiguring your CommOrb to compensate for any camouflage magics.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Please tell me you have a reading on his actual location by now, this chasing him around the houses is becoming irritating...

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: No, but every time he escapes you I am shutting down his teleport to a specific area.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: I think we are on the final stretch now [player name]!

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: I will be able to shut his teleports down permanently in 2, perhaps 3, more scans...

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Keep up the chase [player name]! We nearly have him cornered!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh... Out of interest, what am I going to do with an insane super-powerful murderous mage when he's cornered?

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Leave that to me and the Temple Knights [player name].

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Just follow your orders and continue the chase, and his days are numbered.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well okay then, where should I head now?

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Examining readings now...

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Let's see what we managed to snag in the scan this time!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'm on my way – and Solus had better watch out, I am up to here with his annoying tricks!

sections.title: After the Black Knight is defeated

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Okay, we have our scan results...

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: It's some kind of highly magically susceptible rock...

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Isn't that the same rock that you gave that Zamorakian mage in Varrock?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah it is, which means I know where he is, and how to get there!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's a mine for Rune Essence, but there are only supposed to be a few people who know how to get there...

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Let's worry about that later; Go get Solus!

lines.speaker: Solus Dellagar
lines.text: Well you ARE a persistent [fellow/madam] aren't you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am here to take you in Solus. You can forget your little tricks.

lines.speaker: Solus Dellagar
lines.text: Little tricks, you say? Well how about this one!

lines.speaker: Solus
lines.text: Say hello to my little friend! And goodbye to me!

lines.speaker: Black Knight
lines.text: Solus commands you to die now!

sections.title: Rune Essence Mine: Ending the hunt

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Now that Solus is defeated you should take his hat to Sir Amik to prove that you were responsible.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Now let's head back...

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: ...and may I say:

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: VERY well done, [player name]. Very well done indeed.

sections.title: Communicating with Savant after defeating Solus

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Hello there Player. What can I help you with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi Savant. Where am I supposed to be looking for Solus again?

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: You're not looking for Solus Player, you are supposed to be taking his hat to Sir Amik of the White Knights as proof that you have defeated him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I lost his hat...

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: I have sent you a duplicate, try not to lose it.

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Go and take it to Sir Amik so that he can make you a full-fledged White Knight!

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: There he is!

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Okay, I've blocked all exits from this place except for Temple Knight teleports...

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: I think we finally have him!

lines.speaker: Solus Dellagar
lines.text: Argh! I am so sick of your interference!

lines.speaker: Solus Dellagar
lines.text: I think it is time we end this once and for all!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Agreed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: In the name of the White Knights of Falador, and of the Temple Knights of Saradomin, I am placing you under arrest!

lines.speaker: Solus Dellagar
lines.text: Oh yes?

lines.speaker: Solus Dellagar
lines.text: You and what army?

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: This one, Solus!

lines.speaker: Solus Dellagar
lines.text: Ha!

lines.speaker: Solus Dellagar
lines.text: You'll never take me alive!

lines.speaker: Solus Dellagar
lines.text: You... You meddlesome... You meddlesome freak!

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Player, Solus is very weakened, and we don't have any more fighters to spare!

lines.speaker: Savant
lines.text: Take him out now! This might be your only chance!

lines.speaker: Solus
lines.text: YOU DIE NOW!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I did it Tiffy! I brought Solus in for capture!

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Had a little help doing it though, didn't you, what?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes... But I did all the legwork...

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Haw Haw Haw!

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: That you did Initiate, that you did.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Now take that hat of Solus over to Sir Amik, and we can begin to process your application form as a Temple Knight again, what?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay Tiffy, I'll be back in a jiffy!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, Sir Amik.

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: Hello, friend.

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: You are supposed to be looking for Solus Dellagar!

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: The insane magician who is loose once more upon these lands? Remember?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But... I've already done that!

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: You have??? Then you must have some proof of your encounter!

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: A weapon perhaps, or an item of clothing?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well of course I do! I mean I did...Look, I lost it okay? Stop hassling me!

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: Look, I am very sorry Player, but if you have no proof of your encounter, I can hardly promote you to be a full White Knight, now can I?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh man...I think I've messed up...Maybe Savant can help me out somehow? I'd better speak to her.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well of course I do! I just happened to think it would be a good idea to leave it in the bank for safe keeping.

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: Well bring it here so that I can authenticate your claim and make you a White Knight!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well of course I do! I have it right here!

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: Well hand it over then, and let's see it!

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: Now that you are a White Knight I should perhaps explain a little about how our system works.

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: Sadly we are not as rich as we once were, and there are many White Knights who foolishly lose their combat equipment.

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: We do not think it fair to make a profit from our brethren, so we will sell you equipment at cost, and rebuy it at the same cost, but we will only sell equipment to those we consider responsible enough to

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: wield it correctly.

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: By killing Black Knights, you will increase your reputation with us, by killing White Knights we will obviously think less of you.

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: You can check your White Knight reputation level by looking at your quest journal for the Wanted! Quest, or Sir Vyvin will let you know what level you are at when you go to purchase equipment.

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: Sir Vyvin can be found in Falador Castle, and he will sell you any equipment appropriate to your reputation level.

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: Have fun, and go kill some Black Knights for me!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay Amik, thanks for explaining!
